div.h1: Ring Index Arithmetic — Split Ownership Model
div.p: Power-of-2 sizing enables bitwise AND for modulo-free wrapping
Ring size is always power of 2 (e.g., 256, 1024) Instead of expensive modulo (%): c index = (tail) % entries; // Multi-cycle division
Use bitwise AND (Single CPU cycle): c ring_mask = entries - 1; // e.g., 256-1 = 0xFF index = tail & ring_mask; // AND instruction
Key Architected Constraint: Each index has exactly ONE writer thread. This eliminates the need for Atomic RMW (Lock CMPXCHG) instructions, relying solely on memory ordering primitives.

ul.li: smp_store_release(p, v): Ensures all memory operations prior to this call are globally visible before *p = v is observed by other CPUs.
smp_load_acquire(p): Ensures memory operations after this call do not start until the load from p is complete and values are synchronized.

Platform Implementation:
x86_64: Strong TSO model. Compiles to simple move + compiler barrier.
ARM64/AArch64: Weak model. Compiles to stlr / ldar or dmb ish.
